Friday's volume was on-baseline. The real problems are downstream of screening: `desk_reviews` haven't run in 11 days, `decision_tracking` is empty, and one active position (WTW) is already trading **below its stop-loss** without exit. See §4 and §6.

P&L is in **percent points only** — `desk_positions.position_size` exists but no dollar P&L is computed downstream, so absolute exposure is invisible from the schema alone.

Headline: **DR overrides PM=BUY to AVOID in ~38% of cases** (25/65 reviewed BUYs). Add the 35 ADJUSTED-down-to-BUY_LIMIT/AVOID cases and the senior reviewer is meaningfully reshaping ~92% of the PM=BUY queue. The PM remains structurally more bullish than DR — calibration gap is persistent, not noise.

### Friday's individual DR cases

Only **1 of 4** Friday BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows received a DR verdict before the screener went idle for the holiday.

| Ticker | PM | DR verdict | DR action | Conv. | PM entry | DR entry | PM SL | DR SL | One-line reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| BJ | BUY | ADJUSTED | BUY | HIGH | 88.0–88.5 | 88.0–88.5 (unchanged) | 83.0 | 83.0 (unchanged) | "Textbook market inefficiency — algorithmic overreaction to an immaterial 10 bps margin compression and an insignificant localized employee fraud; obscures EPS+revenue beat, +9.9% membership-income growth, and a transformative Fitch 'BBB' upgrade." |

DR's ADJUSTED-but-no-actual-changes verdict on BJ is unusual — the entry band, SL, and TP1 are identical to PM's. Either the prompt is logging ADJUSTED even when DR concurs, or the changed field is something not surfaced in our view (e.g., conviction or sell levels). Worth a spot-check.

**Not yet reviewed by DR:** GKOS (BUY_LIMIT, -9.06%), RDDT (BUY_LIMIT, -5.02%), XP (BUY_LIMIT, -5.11%). All three are over 24 hours old and unreviewed because (a) the holiday halted the worker and (b) the long-running BUY_LIMIT DR coverage gap (see §6).

## 4. Owned positions

### ACTIVE (5)

| Ticker | Entry date | Entry $ | Current $ | uPnL % | SL $ | TP $ | HWM $ | Score | Last reviewed | SL headroom |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---:|
| MHVIY | 2026-05-14 | 13.28 | 13.38 | +0.79% | 11.90 | 14.48 | 13.38 | 5.65 | 05-14 | **+11.06%** |
| ACM | 2026-05-12 | 69.95 | 71.26 | +1.87% | 65.80 | 79.27 | 71.26 | 7.81 | 05-14 | +7.66% |
| MSI | 2026-05-08 | 387.64 | 400.16 | +3.23% | 374.00 | 415.00 | 400.16 | 6.47 | 05-14 | +6.54% |
| CART | 2026-05-06 | 40.01 | 39.44 | -1.42% | 37.95 | 44.03 | 40.39 | 7.51 | 05-14 | +3.78% |
| **WTW** | **2026-04-30** | **248.99** | **247.72** | **-0.51%** | **253.50** | **265.00** | **259.91** | **7.31** | **05-14** | **−2.33% 🚨** |

**🚨 WTW is the day's most important flag.** Current price ($247.72) is already **below** the configured stop-loss ($253.50). At any meaningful price-tracker cadence this position should have been exited as STOP_LOSS days ago — but `desk_reviews` haven't run since 05-14 and `decision_tracking` is empty (§6), so the trigger never fires. CART is the next-closest (3.78% headroom), below the 5% comfort band but above the explicit 2% flag.

`current_price` and `unrealized_pnl_pct` on every active row carry the **same `last_reviewed_at` timestamp from 2026-05-14** — meaning prices shown above are **11 days stale**. Real-life uPnL and SL distances may be materially different from what the table claims.

**Exit-reason calibration:**

| Reason | n | Avg rPnL % |
|---|---:|---:|
| TAKE_PROFIT | 15 | +6.28% |
| DESK_DECISION | 18 | -0.36% |
| STOP_LOSS | 6 | -5.77% |
| TRAILING_STOP | 1 | -8.74% |

TAKE_PROFIT is doing real work (clear positive expectancy). DESK_DECISION exits cluster near zero — fine, but the line is thin between "saved a bad trade" and "cut a winner early." STOP_LOSS hits at an average of -5.77% suggests SL placement is roughly aligned with the -5 to -8% range we'd expect for a tight dip strategy.

## 5. Friday's full screening haul (2026-05-22, 20 names)

Sector fields are **97% NULL** so the cluster view below is inferred from ticker / region heuristics (see §6 — sector enrichment is broken).

### Cluster A — Foreign large-caps / ADRs (12 names, all AVOID)

Japanese conglomerates: **NECPY**, **KWHIY**, **PTAIY**, **JBAXY**, **YKLTY** (PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA)
Chinese ADRs: **FUTU** (-24.9%, AVOID), **MPNGY** (Meituan, AVOID), **NIO** (PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA)
European: **VONOY** (German RE, AVOID), **CRARY** (Italian industrial, AVOID — **duplicated row id 561/562**)
LatAm telco / industrials: **VIV** (Brazil telco, AVOID)
Other: **NNFSF** (WATCH)

All 12 names ran on `DEEP_DIP` tier except NNFSF (`STANDARD_DIP`). None had `ai_score`, none had `sector` populated, none reached DR. This is a familiar pattern — ADR / OTC tickers route to AVOID/PASS because the data layer doesn't return clean fundamentals.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

| Severity | Flag | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| **HIGH** | **WTW trading below stop-loss without exit** | Active position last_reviewed 2026-05-14; current $247.72 vs SL $253.50 (−2.33%). No `desk_review` since, and `decision_tracking` is empty — the trigger that should have closed this position has not fired. Manual check ASAP. |
| **HIGH** | **`decision_tracking` table is empty (0 rows)** | The post-decision price-tracking loop has produced **zero** rows lifetime. This is the same scaffold whose absence is hiding the WTW stop-loss. Affects every downstream metric: 1/2/4/8-week recovery evaluation, feedback loop into the PM, accuracy backtests. |
| **HIGH** | **`desk_reviews` have not run in 11 days** | Most recent review 2026-05-14T19:36 across all 5 ACTIVE positions. Identical timestamp clustering suggests one bulk batch then total silence. All `current_price` values on the active book are 11-day stale. |
| **HIGH** | **`ai_score` is 100% NULL in last 14 days** (98.2% lifetime, 550/560) | Whatever produced ai_score for the original 10 rows has been off since at least April. Either retire the column or wire it back in — currently it's surfaced in tables and dashboards but conveys no signal. |
| **HIGH** | **`sector` is 98.6% NULL** (552/560) | Only 8 rows have a sector tag; this audit had to fall back to ticker-pattern heuristics for §5. Sector enrichment service appears broken or never wired into the writer. |
| **HIGH** | **66 decision_points with `status='Owned'` have no matching `desk_positions` row** | Orphan flags. Includes recent ones (BJ id=544, EDU 543, WMT 542, NU 472, PODD 440) and three test rows (`TEST` 367, `TEST_T3` 368, `TEST_T12` 375 — all flagged Owned). Either the `desk_positions` write path is stale or `decision_points.status` is being flipped manually/scripted without bookkeeping. BJ in particular is on Friday's slate, so this is a *live* drift. |
| **HIGH** | **MHVIY desk_position is ACTIVE but its `decision_points.status` = 'Not Owned'** | The two tables disagree about the same position. Mirror image of the orphan problem above. |
| **MEDIUM** | **DR coverage on lifetime BUY_LIMIT only 35%** | 42 of 65 BUY_LIMIT rows never received any DR verdict (BUY: 4/69 never reviewed). Coverage gap looks systematic on BUY_LIMIT — queue priority or worker filter may be skipping them. |
| **MEDIUM** | **BJ DR ADJUSTED with no field changes** | DR verdict is ADJUSTED but entry/SL/TP1 identical to PM. Either the parser is mis-labeling CONFIRMED-equivalent runs as ADJUSTED, or the changed field (conviction / sell levels) isn't being surfaced in our tooling. |
| **MEDIUM** | **`batch_comparisons` last run 2026-05-08 (17 days ago)** | Tournament/batch comparison loop has gone quiet. May be expected if the harness is on-demand, but if it's meant to be regular this is drift. |
| **MEDIUM** | **Same-day duplicate decision_points** | CRARY twice on 2026-05-22 (rows 561+562, 2 minutes apart, identical drop_percent). Lifetime: 12 distinct (symbol, date) pairs have ≥2 entries (CAR 3x on 2026-04-25, plus 11 pairs). Idempotency check on the screener insert path is missing. |
| **MEDIUM** | **3 TEST symbols (TEST, TEST_T3, TEST_T12) still flagged status='Owned'** | Synthetic data is contaminating "Owned" counts (110 - 3 = 107 real). Drop or quarantine. |
| **LOW** | **`subscribers` table empty** | No active mailing list. Email service still starts as a background task at FastAPI startup. If intentional, fine — if not, the daily summary has no recipients. |
| **LOW** | **`news_shadow_runs` table empty** | Shadow-model comparison harness has produced zero rows. Either never enabled or not writing back. |
| **LOW** | **3 lifetime DR `ERROR_PARSING` rows** | HQY (04-10), PPERF (04-11), ADBE (04-23). All from early April, none recent — appears to be an old failure mode the prompt has since handled. Worth retiring the rows or re-running them. |
| **LOW** | **No decision_points on 2026-05-25 (today)** | Expected: US Memorial Day, markets closed. Flagging only because the scheduled audit pipeline doesn't auto-detect holidays — silent zero-volume days will need manual confirmation against the trading calendar. |
| **LOW** | **`status` field has no `PENDING` value** | Spec mentions stuck PENDING decision_points as a check; in this DB the only values are `Owned`/`Not Owned`. The PENDING-staleness check is therefore vacuous against the current schema and could be removed from the audit unless the writer is expected to introduce it. |

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **WTW is the day's only true incident.** A 14-day-old position is below stop without a close — that's not a small-sample-noise issue, it's the system failing its core job. Everything else is structural drift; this is operational.
- **The closed book actually works.** 22 W / 18 L with TAKE_PROFIT averaging +6.28% and STOP_LOSS averaging -5.77% is the shape of a coherent dip strategy with R:R roughly 1.1:1 by exit reason. The headline +44.27 pp realized is real even if absolute dollars are invisible.
- **PM is structurally more bullish than DR, and DR is earning its keep.** 25/65 PM=BUY rows were killed to AVOID. If DR went offline tomorrow we'd be running a 12 p.p. lower-quality book. Worth quantifying via backtest once `decision_tracking` is fixed.
- **The screener layer is the healthy part of the pipeline.** Daily volume sits in a tight 10–29 band, gatekeeper tiers are populating, drop_percent and timestamps are clean. The brokenness all clusters in **enrichment** (sector, ai_score), **tracking** (decision_tracking, desk_reviews cadence), and **state-sync** (decision_points.status vs desk_positions).
- **Friday's BUY_LIMIT trio (GKOS, RDDT, XP) is structurally at risk of being silently abandoned.** They sit in the BUY_LIMIT DR-coverage gap (35% lifetime). Without a manual nudge, the Memorial Day pause likely flushes them.
- **Sector enrichment failure has a downstream cost beyond audit aesthetics:** the council debate prompts cite sector context, and the audit cluster view can only be inferred from ticker patterns. Fixing this is small effort, high signal.
- **The `Owned`-status / `desk_positions` drift (66 orphans) is the kind of bug that quietly invalidates win-rate math.** Today's 22 W / 18 L is computed from `desk_positions` only — anything sitting in the 66 orphan set is invisible to win/loss totals.
- **The holiday gap exposed a missing safety:** the audit checked the DB and saw silence; with no calendar awareness, an actual scanner outage on a real trading day would look identical. Worth adding a market-calendar guard before the report is judged "healthy."

## 8. Suggested next actions

Prioritised by impact / effort.

1. **(now)** Manually verify WTW's live price and exit if SL is breached. Don't wait for `desk_reviews` to come back online.
2. **(this week)** Restart the `desk_reviews` worker / cron. 11-day silence across 5 ACTIVE positions is the biggest operational risk in the system right now.
3. **(this week)** Restart whatever populates `decision_tracking`. This is the highest-leverage fix — it unblocks backtesting, the feedback loop, and the SL/TP trigger that just failed on WTW.
4. **(this week)** Trigger DR manually on GKOS, RDDT, XP before they age out of relevance.
5. **(this week)** Reconcile the 66 orphan `status='Owned'` rows. Either backfill missing `desk_positions` or flip those decision_points back to `Not Owned`. Delete the 5 TEST rows or mark them with a sentinel status that's excluded from reporting.
6. **(next sprint)** Investigate why `ai_score` and `sector` are 98–100% NULL. These are referenced in agent prompts and dashboards — the system is operating without two pieces of context it expects to have.
7. **(next sprint)** Add idempotency to the screener insert (composite unique on (symbol, date) within some window) to stop intra-day duplicates like CRARY 561/562.
8. **(next sprint)** Audit why BUY_LIMIT DR coverage is so much lower than BUY (35% vs 94%). Likely a queue-priority asymmetry the senior reviewer doc describes — verify intentional vs bug.
9. **(low priority)** Add a US-trading-calendar check at the top of this audit so holiday silence is detected and labelled automatically rather than treated as zero-volume.
10. **(low priority)** Investigate BJ's ADJUSTED-without-changes verdict to confirm DR's classifier isn't over-firing ADJUSTED on what are functionally CONFIRMED runs.
